title: The Loving Embrace of the Milky Way
gist: Using Chandra and XMM-Newton X-ray data, NASA researchers concluded the Milky Way's outermost spiral arms are spread wider and farther out than previous galactic models assumed.

insights.claim: NASA researchers using Chandra X-ray Observatory and ESA's XMM-Newton data concluded the Milky Way's outermost spiral arms are spread farther and wider than earlier models calculated.
insights.speaker: Bob Grant
insights.so_what: Revises the basic geometry of our own galaxy.

insights.claim: The new galactic-arm measurements were derived from distances between dust clouds in the outer Milky Way and from rings around gamma-ray bursts emitted by collapsing or merging stars.
insights.speaker: Bob Grant
insights.so_what: Illustrates the indirect distance-measurement methods behind the finding.
